'''''UFC 320: Ankalaev vs. Pereira 2''''' was a [[mixed martial arts]] event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] that took place on October 4, 2025, at the [[T-Mobile Arena]] in [[Paradise, Nevada]], part of the [[Las Vegas Valley]], United States.<ref name="LV">{{Cite web|url=https://www.sportsnet.ca/ufc/article/ufc-320-announced-for-october-at-t-mobile-arena-in-las-vegas/|title=UFC 320 announced for October at T-Mobile Arena in Las Vegas|publisher=sportsnet.ca|author=Staff|date=2025-06-07|access-date=2025-06-07}}</ref>

==Background==
This event was originally expected to take place in [[Guadalajara]], Mexico  on September 13 and serve as the third annual "Noche UFC" event commemorating [[Independence Day (Mexico)|Mexico's independence day]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2025/03/ufc-320-noche-guadalajara-mexico-announcement|title=Noche UFC heads to Mexico for first time as UFC 320 set for Guadalajara|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2025-03-29|access-date=2025-03-29}}</ref> However, due to the construction of Arena Guadalajara being stalled, the card was moved to [[San Antonio, Texas]] and shifted to a [[UFC Fight Night: Lopes vs. Silva|Fight Night]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://cagesidepress.com/2025/05/31/noche-ufc-moved-from-guadalajara-to-san-antonio-texas/|title=Noche UFC moved from Guadalajara to San Antonio, Texas|publisher=cagesidepress.com|author=Eddie Law|date=2025-05-31|access-date=2025-05-31}}</ref> The promotion announced a week later that the numbered event would be moved to October and took place in Las Vegas.<ref name="LV"/>

A [[UFC Light Heavyweight Championship]] rematch between current champion [[Magomed Ankalaev]] and former champion [[Alex Pereira]] (also former [[List of UFC champions#Middleweight Championship|UFC Middleweight Champion]], former [[Glory (kickboxing)#Glory Middleweight Championship|Glory Middleweight]] and [[Glory (kickboxing)#Glory Light Heavyweight Championship|Light Heavyweight Champion]]) headlined the event.<ref name="main">{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmamania.com/2025/7/22/24472558/ufc-320-ankalaev-vs-pereira-2-main-event-set-merab-vs-sandhagen-co-headlines-las-vegas-card|title=UFC 320: Ankalaev vs. Pereira 2 main event set, Merab vs. Sandhagen co-headlines Las Vegas card|publisher=mmamania.com|author=Alexander Behunin|date=2025-07-22|access-date=2025-07-22}}</ref> They previously competed at [[UFC 313]] where Ankalaev defeated Pereira for the championship via unanimous decision.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://cagesidepress.com/2025/03/09/ufc-313-magomed-ankalaev-rains-on-poatans-parade-dethrones-alex-pereira/|title=UFC 313: Magomed Ankalaev Rains on Poatan’s Parade, Dethrones Alex Pereira|publisher=cagesidepress.com|author=Jay Anderson|date=2025-03-09|access-date=2025-03-09}}</ref> Former champion (also the inaugural [[Rizin Fighting Federation#Light Heavyweight Championship|Rizin Light Heavyweight Champion]]) [[Jiří Procházka]], who met former title challenger [[Khalil Rountree Jr.]] at the event, served as backup and potential replacement for this fight.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2025/7/28/24476157/jiri-prochazka-set-to-back-up-magomed-ankalaev-vs-alex-pereira-2-at-ufc-320|title=Jiri Prochazka set to back up Magomed Ankalaev vs. Alex Pereira 2 at UFC 320|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2025-07-28|access-date=2025-07-28}}</ref>

In addition, a [[UFC Bantamweight Championship]] bout between current champion [[Merab Dvalishvili]] and former interim title challenger [[Cory Sandhagen]] co-headlined the event.<ref name="main"/>

A featherweight bout between [[Daniel Santos (fighter)|Daniel Santos]] and Yoo Joo-sang was scheduled for [[UFC Fight Night: Ulberg vs. Reyes]] a week before, but it was eventually moved to this event due to undisclosed reasons.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.sportsnet.ca/ufc/article/ufc-perth-adds-three-more-australian-fighters-to-fight-night-card/|title=UFC Perth adds three more Australian fighters to Fight Night card|publisher=sportsnet.ca|author=Staff|date=2025-08-26|access-date=2026-03-26}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/ufc/406620/daniel-santos-wants-to-flatline-zombie-jr-at-ufc-320-for-first-knockout-in-4-years|title=Daniel Santos wants to flatline Zombie Jr. at UFC 320 for first knockout in 4 years|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Guilherme Cruz|date=2025-10-05|access-date=2026-03-26}}</ref>

[[Ateba Gautier]] and former [[Legacy Fighting Alliance#LFA Middleweight Championship|LFA Middleweight Champion]] Osman Diaz were scheduled to meet in a middleweight bout at this event.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2025-07-23|title=UFC 318 star Ateba Gautier reportedly books UFC 320 return just days after brutal knockout win |url=https://bloodyelbow.com/2025/07/23/ufc-318-star-ateba-gautier-reportedly-books-ufc-320-return-just-days-after-brutal-knockout-win/|access-date=2025-07-23|publisher=bloodyelbow.com|author=Curtis Calhoun}}</ref> However, Diaz pulled out from the event due to a medical issue and was replaced by promotional newcomer Tre'ston Vines.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Nolan King|title=Fighter withdraws from UFC 320 six days prior to pay-per-view event|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2025/09/29/ufc-320-ozzy-diaz-vs-ateba-gautier-withdrawal/86430098007/|access-date=2025-09-29|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Ateba Gautier receives new opponent, remains on UFC 320 card|url=https://www.sherdog.com/news/news/Ateba-Gautier-Receives-New-Opponent-Remains-on-UFC-320-Card-198538|date=2025-09-30|access-date=2025-10-01|publisher=sherdog.com|author=Tristen Critchfield}}</ref>

At the weigh-ins, [[The Ultimate Fighter: Heavy Hitters]] featherweight winner [[Macy Chiasson]] weighed in at 137.5 pounds, one and a half pounds over the women's bantamweight non-title fight limit. The bout proceeded at catchweight and she was fined a percentage of her purse, which went to her opponent former [[Invicta Fighting Championships#Bantamweight Championship|Invicta FC Bantamweight Champion]] and [[UFC Women's Featherweight Championship]] challenger [[Yana Santos]].<ref name="weighins">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2025/10/03/ufc-320-ankalaev-pereira-dvalishvili-sandhagen-title-weigh-in-results-live-video-stream/86481602007/|title=UFC 320 weigh-in results: One heavy in Vegas, but title fighters on point|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Staff|date=2025-10-03|access-date=2025-10-03}}</ref> The featherweight bout between [[Daniel Santos (fighter)|Daniel Santos]] and Yoo Joo-sang was also changed to a catchweight bout of 153 pounds.<ref name="weighins"/>

==Event==
===Main card===
====Dvalishvili vs. Sandhagen ====
The fight was predicted by most outlets to be another win by Dvalishvili, noting his wrestling expertise and impressive cardio. However, Sandhagen's striking ability and aggression was noted as potentially being able to change the tide of the fight.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Andrew Richardson|date=2025-10-03|title=Merab Dvalishvili vs. Cory Sandhagen: Odds, full fight preview and prediction|url=https://sports.yahoo.com/article/merab-dvalishvili-vs-cory-sandhagen-130500790.html|access-date=2025-10-25|publisher=sports.yahoo.com}}</ref>

The bout started out with a close round where Sandhagen saw some success against Dvalishvili. Dvalishvili controlled the bout against Sandhagen starting in the second round, dropping Sandhagen and landing over 8 takedowns over 13 attempts. Dvalishvili later expressed surprise he could not finish Sandhagen in the second round, where he landed over 33 strikes compared to just 2 by Sandhagen.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2025-10-05|title=Dvalishvili's reign continues in rout of Sandhagen|url=https://www.espn.com/mma/story/_/id/46493053/dvalishvili-rolls-decisive-win-sandhagen-ufc-320|access-date=2025-10-25|publisher=espn.com|author=Jeff Wagenheim}}</ref>

====Ankalaev vs. Pereira====
Before the fight, many commentators and sites predicted a similar result to the previous fight, where Ankalaev out-struck Pereira standing up.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Magomed Ankalaev vs. Alex Pereira prediction: Who takes rematch at UFC 320?|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2025/10/03/ufc-320-magomed-ankalaev-vs-alex-pereira-pick-odds-time/86497554007/|date=2025-10-03|access-date=2025-10-18|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Dan Tom}}</ref> Pereira claimed that he only fought at "40 percent" of his strength in the previous fight, dealing with an injured hand.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2025-09-27|title=Pereira says he fought Ankalaev at '40 percent'|url=https://www.espn.com/mma/story/_/id/46391411/pereira-says-was-40-percent-ufc-title-loss-ankalaev|access-date=2025-10-20|publisher=espn.com|author=Brett Okamoto}}</ref>

Contrary to predictions of a close match, the fight saw Pereira immediately dominating Ankalaev, backing him to the fence and attacking him with calf kicks and a right hand.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2025-10-05|title=UFC 320 results and analysis for Ankalaev vs. Pereira 2|url=https://www.espn.com/mma/story/_/id/46453040/ufc-320-live-results-analysis-magomed-ankalaev-alex-pereira-merab-dvalishvili-cory-sandhagen|access-date=2025-10-18|publisher=espn.com|author=Staff}}</ref> After being hit with a right hand, Ankalaev unsuccessfully shot for a takedown, leading Pereira to bombard him with elbows and punches until the fight was ruled a technical knockout eighty seconds in.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2025-10-05|title=UFC 320 -- Magomed Ankalaev vs. Alex Pereira 2: Results, winners, highlights, fight card, complete guide|url=https://www.cbssports.com/mma/news/ufc-320-magomed-ankalaev-vs-alex-pereira-2-results-winners-highlights-fight-card-complete-guide/|access-date=2025-10-18|publisher=cbssports.com|author=Shakiel Mahjouri}}</ref>

== Bonus awards ==
The following fighters received $50,000 bonuses.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmamania.com/ufc-320-ankalaev-vs-pereira-rematch-start-time-full-results-espn-mma/393976/ufc-320-bonuses-jiri-prochazka-double-dips-for-100000-off-wild-come-from-behind-knockout|title=UFC 320 bonuses: Jiri Prochazka double dips for $100,000 off wild come from behind knockout|publisher=mmamania.com|author=Ryan Harkness|date=2025-10-05|access-date=2025-10-05}}</ref>
*'''Fight of the Night: Jiří Procházka vs. Khalil Rountree Jr.'''
*'''Performance of the Night: Alex Pereira''', '''Jiří Procházka''', and '''Joe Pyfer'''

==Aftermath==
This event won the [[2025 in UFC#2025 UFC Honors awards|UFC Honors ''2025 Event of the Year'']] award as voted by the fans.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.facebook.com/watch/?v=2362923857500828|title=UFC 320 is our winner for #UFCHonors Event of the Year!|publisher=facebook.com|author=UFC|date=2025-01-14|access-date=2026-01-18}}</ref>
